Name: Conditional Access policies contain private IP addresses
Description:
This indicator checks if any named locations contains private IP addresses and are assigned to enabled Conditional Access policies.

Required permissions

  • Policy.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Named locations should define public egress IP addresses or ranges, as Entra ID will see the public address of a user when evaluating Conditional Access policies.

If a private IP address or range is defined in a named location, it may result in the associated Conditional Access policies not operating as intended. Depending on how the policy is defined, it may present a security posture lower than intended.

The attack surface would be specific to the intention of the impacted Conditional Access policy.

References

Using networks and countries/regions in Microsoft Entra ID - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: There are Conditional Access policies referencing named locations containing private IP Addresses. These private addresses will not be considered while access is determined.
Remediation:
Review the impacted Conditional Access policy, and verify the policy conditions are working as expected.

If the policy is working as expected, the Named location should be removed from the policy, as private IP addresses or ranges are not used in Conditional Access evaluation.

If the policy is not working as expected and is designed to evaluate based on network location, the Named location should be changed to reflect the egress IP addresses or ranges.

Organizations must ensure that all Conditional Access policy changes are documented and verify that they do not cause unintended negative impact

Named locations can be reviewed in the Entra admin center (direct link). To browse to them, on the left menu, expand Protection and then expand Conditional Access. In the blade on the right, select Named locations.
